headline: Inflation data week arrives as Iran ceasefire holds and rate-hike odds top 57%. May CPI and PPI are the fulcrum — hot prints reopen the hike trade.
sub: May nonfarm payrolls blew past estimates at 172K, sending the 10-year yield to 4.54% and sparking a 'good news is bad news' selloff that dragged the S&P 500 back from its record 7,600 close. The Iran war's energy shock pushed April CPI to near two-year highs, and this week's May reads will determine whether pipeline inflation is easing or entrenching. New Fed Chair Kevin Warsh's first press conference lands June 17 — this week's data will write his opening script.

events.detail: Most critical inflation print of 2026 — energy base effects from Iran war shock in focus; April print was highest in nearly two years
Uneasy truce holds but shipping at just 5% of pre-war levels; any escalation re-spikes crude and stagflation risk
Producer prices complete the inflation picture ahead of Warsh's June 17 debut presser
First major central bank move since Iran ceasefire; guidance sets EUR/USD tone and signals global policy direction
Post-payrolls repricing — watch 10-year yield for further pressure on rate-sensitive growth stocks
Household inflation expectations running hotter than forecaster models; a beat here adds to hike fears

bias: Tactically cautious into the inflation prints. Hot CPI = yields spike, growth sells off hard; cool CPI = relief rally, tech and semis lead. Asymmetric risk is to the upside on yields given the Strait of Hormuz energy overhang — fade strength in rate-sensitive sectors until Wednesday's data clears the air.
